title: Container.labels can return None
body:
`Container.labels` can return `None` if no labels were attached to the container. This goes against the property's description where it claims to always return a `dict`:

https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/7a674914edb15b2c3e657e74f0eca778a66eb9bc/podman/domain/containers.py#L42-L49

The reason this happens is because podman sets a lack of labels as `null` rather than an empty `dict`. This can be easily verified with the following command:

```bash
podman create quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest | xargs podman inspect | jq '.[].Config.Labels'
```
